# Binboi

> Binboi is a self-hosted tunneling tool. It exposes local services (HTTP, HTTPS, TCP) to the internet via public URLs with automatic TLS, a request inspector, webhook replay, and team management — all under your own infrastructure.

## What Binboi does

- Creates encrypted tunnels from a public URL to a local port (e.g. `localhost:3000`)
- Provisions TLS certificates automatically via ACME (Let's Encrypt or custom CA)
- Provides a local request inspector at `http://localhost:4040` — live request/response viewer with replay
- Supports TCP tunnels for databases, SSH, and raw TCP services
- Ships first-class SDKs for JavaScript/Node.js, Python, and Rust
- Is entirely self-hosted: you run the server on your own VPS; your traffic never touches a third party

## CLI quick reference

```bash
# Install
npm install -g @binboi/cli

# Authenticate
binboi login --server https://tunnel.example.com
binboi login --token YOUR_TOKEN --server https://tunnel.example.com

# HTTP tunnel
binboi http 3000
binboi http 3000 --subdomain myapp
binboi http 3000 --hostname dev.mycompany.com
binboi http 3000 --region eu-west
binboi http 3000 --basic-auth "user:pass"
binboi http 3000 --allow-cidr 10.0.0.0/8
binboi http 3000 --no-https-redirect
binboi http 3000 --inspect-port 4041

# TCP tunnel
binboi tcp 5432
binboi tcp 5432 --remote-port 15432
binboi tcp 22 --subdomain myssh

# TLS passthrough tunnel (no termination)
binboi tls 443

# Start all tunnels defined in config file
binboi start --all
binboi start frontend backend

# Replay captured requests
binboi replay last
binboi replay req_abc123
binboi replay req_abc123 --header "X-Debug: true"

# Diagnostics
binboi ping
binboi ping --all-regions
binboi tunnels list
binboi tunnels stop <tunnel-id>
binboi config show
binboi diagnose

# TLS management
binboi tls renew --hostname dev.mycompany.com
binboi tls status --hostname dev.mycompany.com

# Server (provider) commands
binboi server logs --follow
binboi server status
```

## REST API (base: /api/v1)

All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` unless noted.

### Tunnels
- `GET    /api/v1/tunnels`               — list active tunnels for the authenticated user
- `POST   /api/v1/tunnels`               — create a new tunnel session
- `GET    /api/v1/tunnels/{id}`          — get tunnel by ID
- `DELETE /api/v1/tunnels/{id}`          — close a tunnel
- `GET    /api/v1/tunnels/{id}/requests` — list captured requests for a tunnel

### Requests
- `GET    /api/v1/requests`              — list recent captured requests (all tunnels)
- `GET    /api/v1/requests/{id}`         — get full request/response detail
- `POST   /api/v1/requests/{id}/replay`  — replay a captured request

### Authentication
- `POST   /api/v1/auth/login`            — exchange credentials for a token (no auth header)
- `POST   /api/v1/auth/logout`           — revoke current token
- `GET    /api/v1/tokens`                — list all tokens for the authenticated user
- `POST   /api/v1/tokens`                — create a new token
- `DELETE /api/v1/tokens/{id}`           — revoke a token by ID

### Team (admin only)
- `GET    /api/v1/team/members`          — list team members
- `POST   /api/v1/team/members`          — invite a member / issue a token
- `DELETE /api/v1/team/members/{id}`     — remove a member

### Server
- `GET    /api/v1/health`                — server health check (no auth required)
- `GET    /api/v1/metrics`               — Prometheus-format metrics (no auth required)

## SDK usage

### JavaScript / Node.js
```typescript
import { Binboi } from '@binboi/sdk';

const client = new Binboi({ token: process.env.BINBOI_TOKEN });
const tunnel = await client.http({ addr: 3000, subdomain: 'myapp' });
console.log(tunnel.url); // https://myapp.tunnel.example.com
await tunnel.close();
```

### Python
```python
import binboi
client = binboi.Client()
with client.http(addr=3000, subdomain="myapp") as tunnel:
    print(tunnel.url)
```

### Rust
```rust
use binboi::Client;
let client = Client::from_env().await?;
let tunnel = client.http().addr(3000).subdomain("myapp").start().await?;
println!("{}", tunnel.url());
```

## Key concepts

- **Tunnel** — a persistent, multiplexed connection from the Binboi server to the CLI on your machine. Incoming requests are forwarded through this connection to your local port.
- **Subdomain** — the `<name>` in `<name>.binboi.dev`. Can be random (auto-assigned) or reserved (stable across sessions).
- **Inspector** — the local HTTP dashboard at `localhost:4040` that shows every request/response for running tunnels.
- **Region** — the geographic edge node where tunnel connections are accepted. Choosing the closest region to your users reduces latency.
- **Provider** — the self-hosted Binboi server. Configured via `/etc/binboi/server.yaml`. Requires a public IP, a domain with wildcard DNS, and outbound port 443.
- **Token** — an auth credential. Stored in `~/.binboi/config.json` after `binboi login`. Can also be set via the `BINBOI_TOKEN` environment variable.

## Configuration file (~/.binboi/config.yaml)

```yaml
server: https://tunnel.example.com
token: tok_alice_abc123
region: us-east

tunnels:
  frontend:
    proto: http
    addr: 3000
    subdomain: myapp-frontend
  api:
    proto: http
    addr: 8080
    subdomain: myapp-api
  db:
    proto: tcp
    addr: 5432
    remote_port: 15432
```

## Environment variables

| Variable            | Description                          |
|---------------------|--------------------------------------|
| `BINBOI_TOKEN`      | Auth token (overrides config file)   |
| `BINBOI_SERVER`     | Server URL (overrides config file)   |
| `BINBOI_REGION`     | Default region                       |
| `BINBOI_LOG_LEVEL`  | Log level: debug, info, warn, error  |
| `BINBOI_LOG_FORMAT` | Log format: text, json               |

## Self-hosting (provider)

Run the Binboi server with Docker:

```bash
docker run -d \
  --name binboi-server \
  -p 80:80 -p 443:443 -p 4443:4443 \
  -v /etc/binboi:/etc/binboi \
  -e BINBOI_DOMAIN=tunnel.example.com \
  -e BINBOI_AUTH_TOKEN=change_me \
  ghcr.io/binboi/server:latest
```

Requires:
- DNS: `A tunnel.example.com → <server-ip>` and `A *.tunnel.example.com → <server-ip>`
- Ports 80 and 443 open for inbound (ACME challenge + tunnel connections)

## Links

- Docs: https://binboi.dev/docs
